We recommended Management to direct the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer to observe the perpetual inventory method following the procedures under MNGAS for LGUs and PPSAS 12 to fairly present the Inventories: 

The local accountant shall draw a Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal and in the inventory ledger cards. These Supplies Ledger Cards are subsidiary ledgers maintained by the accounting office for each type of supplies, crops and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal.

The required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports shall be prepared, maintained and submitted for the recognition of the expense portion of supplies inventories.  The Municipal Treasurer shall maintain the Stock Card to be used to record all receipts and issuances of supplies. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which the Municipal Treasurer shall summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuances. The Journal Voucher drawn based on the RSMI recognized the expense and the issuance of the Inventories.

At the end of the year, a Physical Count shall be conducted to determine the undistributed/ unconsumed items and the cost of these items shall be restored back to the inventory account for any necessary adjustment in the books and/or inventory records for proper valuation. The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories shall be used to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date. It shows the balance of inventory items per cards and per count and shortage/overage, if any.
observations.managementAction: It is highly recommended, that a plantilla position of a GSO be created to focus on this matter; The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The accounting office and the treasury office to monitor the in and out of the inventory through proper documentation of RIS and RSMI. The accountant shall recognize an inventory upon purchase and every QUARTER the accountant shall draw JEV to record the inventories issued as per RIS and RSMI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management, the following measures in compliance to the provisions of PPSAS No. 12 and Section 2 of PD 1445: 

The Municipal Accountant to refrain from the practice of directly recording purchased construction materials to Construction-in-Progress accounts and see to it that they are properly recorded to their corresponding inventory account; 

To centralize the handling and monitoring of inventories and supplies of the Municipality thru designating another permanent personnel as General Services Officer, instead of the Municipal Treasurer, whose only task is to oversee and monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories, and maintain the monitoring forms. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which will be summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuance; and

As much as practicable, to provide an area/space or construct an adequate warehouse or storage facility to safe keep all inventories and supplies owned by the Municipality to secure the same from risk of loss or wastage. Best safekeeping means protecting the materials against theft, fire and deterioration but easily accessible when needed. It is advisable to revisit the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGU since it explains the processes such as storage, warehousing, issuance, utilization and inventory taking, among others.
observations.managementAction:
Procured construction material shall be recognized as inventory. Upon issuance and consumption of such, RIS shall be issued as well to support the issuance, and the end of the month the Treasury shall make and RSMI per RIS due to the volume of inventory per RIS, then the RSMI attached with RIS shall be submitted to the accountant as a basis to draw recognition of the appropriate CIP.

During the exit conference, Management commented that the construction of an adequate warehouse or storage facility may not be possible due to lack of funds. 

However, commitment was made as to the centralization in handling and monitoring of construction materials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to direct the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer to observe the perpetual inventory method following the procedures under MNGAS for LGUs and PPSAS 12 to fairly present the Inventories: 

The local accountant shall draw a Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal and in the inventory ledger cards. These Supplies Ledger Cards are subsidiary ledgers maintained by the accounting office for each type of supplies, crops and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal.

The required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports shall be prepared, maintained and submitted for the recognition of the expense portion of supplies inventories.  The Municipal Treasurer shall maintain the Stock Card to be used to record all receipts and issuances of supplies. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which the Municipal Treasurer shall summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuances. The Journal Voucher drawn based on the RSMI recognized the expense and the issuance of the Inventories.

At the end of the year, a Physical Count shall be conducted to determine the undistributed/ unconsumed items and the cost of these items shall be restored back to the inventory account for any necessary adjustment in the books and/or inventory records for proper valuation. The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories shall be used to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date. It shows the balance of inventory items per cards and per count and shortage/overage, if any
observations.managementAction:
The accounting office and the treasury office to monitor the in and out of the inventory through proper documentation of RIS and RSMI. The accountant shall recognize an inventory upon purchase and every QUARTER the accountant shall draw JEV to record the inventories issued as per RIS and RSMI.

Every quarter, the treasury office would prepare RIS and RSMI of the inventories issued, and would submit this report to the accounting office as reference for the journal entry.
observations.reason: The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management, the following measures in compliance to the provisions of PPSAS No. 12 and Section 2 of PD 1445:

The Municipal Accountant to refrain from the practice of directly recording purchased construction materials to Construction-in-Progress accounts and see to it that they are properly recorded to their corresponding inventory account; 

To centralize the handling and monitoring of inventories and supplies of the Municipality thru designating another permanent personnel as General Services Officer, instead of the Municipal Treasurer, whose only task is to oversee and monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories, and maintain the monitoring forms. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which will be summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuance; and

As much as practicable, to provide an area/space or construct an adequate warehouse or storage facility to safe keep all inventories and supplies owned by the Municipality to secure the same from risk of loss or wastage. Best safekeeping means protecting the materials against theft, fire and deterioration but easily accessible when needed. It is advisable to revisit the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGU since it explains the processes such as storage, warehousing, issuance, utilization and inventory taking, among others
observations.managementAction:
Procured construction material shall be recognized as inventory. Upon issuance and consumption of such, RIS shall be issued to support the issuance, and the end of the month the Treasury shall make and RSMI per RIS due to the volume of inventory per RIS, then the RSMI attached with RIS shall be submitted to the accountant as a basis to draw recognition of the appropriate CIP.

Municipal Accountant recognized the purchase of inventory as Construction Inventory.

Upon issuance and consumption of such, RIS was issued to support the issuance. | During the exit conference, Management commented that the construction of an adequate warehouse or storage facility may not be possible due to lack of funds. 

However, commitment was made as to the centralization in handling and monitoring of construction materials.

We recommended Management the following remedial measures:
To require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare an updated Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 379 of RA No. 7160 to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal;
To investigate the loss of some unserviceable properties such as 2 trisikad and a motorcycle; and
To direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to derecognize the unserviceable equipment as prescribed under PPSAS No. 17 after IIRUP is duly approved and evaluated by COA.

We recommended Management the following remedial measures:
To fast track the implementation of the inland fish culture, construction of nurseries and procurement of seedlings, vermi culture, and agricultural demonstration farm (BuB 2014) funded from the national government agency for timely delivery of benefits/services to the intended beneficiaries in compliance to the provisions of the Local Government Code;
To carry out its responsibilities under Section 2 of the MOA particularly to properly manage the fund, and to return to DA-RFO 10 any unutilized amount after the completion of the project; and
To coordinate and to reconcile with DA-RFO 10 on the discrepancies on the unliquidated balances so as to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended Management to monitor and to expedite the implementation of the remaining projects under the 20% LDF to adequately deliver the basic services to the constituents and maximize its benefits to its intended beneficiaries in compliance to the provisions of Section 5 of DILG-DBM Circular No. 2017-01 and RA No. 7160.
We recommended Management to strictly comply and observe the provisions of Section 37 of the Revised IRR of RA 9814, and to submit explanation for the non-compliance thereto.

We recommended to Management the following remedial actions:
To direct the BAC Secretariat to submit the contract documents of 28 infrastructure projects for contract review, and to adopt means to ensure timely submission to COA of any contract entered into by the Municipality, consistent with COA Circular 2009-001; and
To take administrative disciplinary action for any unjustifiable failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with requirements as provided under the same Circular.

We recommended Management to discontinue the processing of claims for fuel used by private vehicles to avoid further disallowance in audit as provided under Section 4(2) of PD 1445, Section 3.1.8 of COA Circular No. 96-004, and Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended Management to disburse repairs and maintenance expenses with attachments such as pre-repair inspection report, post-repair inspection report, report of waste material and history card, and to maintain a checklist of required documents so as to ensure the completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended to Local Chief Executive the following measures in accordance with Section 5 of RA No. 656, Section 21 of RA 10121, and Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2018-002:
To insure the insurable properties with the GSIS following the guidelines on COA Circular No. 2018-002 for the proper indemnification of all insurable properties with the GSIS; and
To appropriate funds for the payment of insurance premiums on the property from the Local DRRMF for the payment of premiums on calamity insurance as prescribed under Section 21 of RA 10121.

We recommended Management the following:
To discontinue granting of loans to employees by the employees’ cooperative of the Municipality if upon assessment of their pay slip, the net take-home pay would become below ₱5,000 as a result of deducting additional loan payments;
To discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private institutions from the personnel’s payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors; and
To ensure that each employee would receive net take-home pay of at least ₱5,000.00/month for CY 2022 pursuant to Section 55 of GAA for FY2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to direct the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer to observe the perpetual inventory method following the procedures under MNGAS for LGUs and PPSAS 12 to fairly present the Inventories: 

The local accountant shall draw a Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal and in the inventory ledger cards. These Supplies Ledger Cards are subsidiary ledgers maintained by the accounting office for each type of supplies, crops and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal.

The required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports shall be prepared, maintained and submitted for the recognition of the expense portion of supplies inventories.  The Municipal Treasurer shall maintain the Stock Card to be used to record all receipts and issuances of supplies. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which the Municipal Treasurer shall summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuances. The Journal Voucher drawn based on the RSMI recognized the expense and the issuance of the Inventories.
At the end of the year, a Physical Count shall be conducted to determine the undistributed/ unconsumed items and the cost of these items shall be restored back to the inventory account for any necessary adjustment in the books and/or inventory records for proper valuation. The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories shall be used to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date. It shows the balance of inventory items per cards and per count and shortage/overage, if any.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 3 | The accounting office and the treasury office to monitor the in and out of the inventory through proper documentation of RIS and RSMI. The accountant shall recognize an inventory upon purchase and every QUARTER the accountant shall draw JEV to record the inventories issued as per RIS and RSMI.
observations.reason: Every quarter, the treasury office would prepare RIS and RSMI of the inventories issued, and would submit this report to the accounting office as reference for the journal entry. | The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management, the following measures in compliance to the provisions of PPSAS No. 12 and Section 2 of PD 1445:

The Municipal Accountant to refrain from the practice of directly recording purchased construction materials to Construction-in-Progress accounts and see to it that they are properly recorded to their corresponding inventory account; 

To centralize the handling and monitoring of inventories and supplies of the Municipality thru designating another permanent personnel as General Services Officer, instead of the Municipal Treasurer, whose only task is to oversee and monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories, and maintain the monitoring forms. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which will be summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuance; and

As much as practicable, to provide an area/space or construct an adequate warehouse or storage facility to safe keep all inventories and supplies owned by the Municipality to secure the same from risk of loss or wastage. Best safekeeping means protecting the materials against theft, fire and deterioration but easily accessible when needed. It is advisable to revisit the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGU since it explains the processes such as storage, warehousing, issuance, utilization and inventory taking, among others.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 4 | Procured construction material shall be recognized as inventory. Upon issuance and consumption of such, RIS shall be issued as well to support the issuance, and the end of the month the Treasury shall make and RSMI per RIS due to the volume of inventory per RIS, then the RSMI attached with RIS shall be submitted to the accountant as a basis to draw recognition of the appropriate CIP.
observations.reason:
Municipal Accountant recognized the purchase of inventory as Construction Inventory. 

Upon issuance and consumption of such, RIS was issued to support the issuance. | During the exit conference, Management commented that the construction of an adequate warehouse or storage facility may not be possible due to lack of funds. 

However, commitment was made as to the centralization in handling and monitoring of construction materials.

The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories shall be used to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date. It shows the balance of inventory items per cards and per count and shortage/overage, if any.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Management, the following measures in compliance to the provisions of PPSAS No. 12 and Section 2 of PD 1445:
The Municipal Accountant to refrain from the practice of directly recording purchased construction materials to Construction-in-Progress accounts and see to it that they are properly recorded to their corresponding inventory account;
To centralize the handling and monitoring of inventories and supplies of the Municipality thru designating another permanent personnel as General Services Officer, instead of the Municipal Treasurer, whose only task is to oversee and monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories, and maintain the monitoring forms. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which will be summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuance; and
As much as practicable, to provide an area/space or construct an adequate warehouse or storage facility to safe keep all inventories and supplies owned by the Municipality to secure the same from risk of loss or wastage. Best safekeeping means protecting the materials against theft, fire and deterioration but easily accessible when needed. It is advisable to revisit the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGU since it explains the processes such as storage, warehousing, issuance, utilization and inventory taking, among others.

Ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all contract submitted to COA for review in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445; and
Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman explained that BAC Personnel are performing other duties aside from BAC concerns thus submission of contract reviews was delayed but assured the Auditor that the submission of documents on time will be observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the loan payments through salary deductions in favor of the private financial institutions for those with minimum net take home pay below P5,000, so as to strictly observe the implementation of the minimum take home pay required under Section 48 of the General Provisions of FY 2018 GAA.
observations.managementAction: The personnel concerned is now regulating the loan approvals of the employees.
observations.reason:
LGU cannot immediately stop the deductions for loan payment of employees due to MOA with Land Bank of the Philippines.

However, said finding was noted favorably, with this the succeeding loans will be regulated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management the following remedial measures:

Discontinue the payment of registration fees  in excess of P2,000 limitation per day per participant for conventions, meetings, seminars and trainings conducted by NGOs and private institutions, in conformity with NBC No. 563, otherwise the excess shall be shouldered by the participant; and
Strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 on the prohibition against excessive and unnecessary expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The management does not allow attendance for trainings & seminar conducted by NGOs & private institutions.
observations.reason:
Some already settled the Notice of Disallowance issued.

An Appeal Memorandum was received by the Office relating to such unnecessary travels of the SB officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to strictly comply with the guidelines set forth in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 series of 2015 in the implementation of the Enhanced AICS program of DSWD. Also, we recommend that the MSWDO should ensure that authority to approve grants beyond P5,000.00 was actually delegated to them.

We recommended that the municipal Accountant should exhaust all possible means to determine the dates of Acquisition of the depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment. In addition, compute and record immediately the deprecation in the books of accounts in order to present fairly value of the Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management is on the process of retrieving data.
observations.reason: Absence of records and manpower constraint contributed to the delay in the implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Development Committee optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through: (a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; (b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result and (c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.
observations.managementAction: Management is still on the process of assessment and monitoring its projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality should install an internal control system on the issuance of gasoline to avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditure for fuel. 

We also recommend that the Municipality should comply with the provisions of COA Circular 77-61 to regulate fuel consumption as well as the use of government vehicles.

We also recommended that management should refrain from providing fuel for privately owned vehicles and that it should require duly accomplished driver’s trip ticket before issuance of gasoline. Should use of private vehicles be inevitable, the same should be justified and the quantity of gasoline used must be reasonable, otherwise, same shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason:
Some trip tickets were not filled up properly.

Strict control on the usage of gasoline is enjoined.

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 on proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste through the following remedial measures:

Carry-out the conversion of the open dump site for solid wastes into controlled dump site, in accordance with the guidelines set in Sec. 41 of RA No. 9003, provided, further, that no controlled dumps shall be allowed five (5) years; and
Ensuring the implementation of segregation at source of solid waste, enforcement of “no segregation, no pick-up” policy with every barangay, and construction of Material Recovery Facilities (MRF) in the urbanized barangays of Poblacion, Punta Silum, Cabalantian and Tu-od, and the development of disposal facility in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) should review and update the Municipality’s 10-year Integrated Solid Waste Management Comprehensive Plan (ISWMCP) to ensure its sustainability, viability, relevance and effectiveness in compliance to RA No. 9003.

We recommended and Management agreed the following remedial measures:
Ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all contract submitted to COA for review in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445; and
Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit for contract review the contract documents of the above-mentioned projects, programs and activities, and employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of any contract entered into by the Municipality, consistent with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the loan payments through salary deductions in favor of the private financial institutions for those with minimum net take home pay below P5,000, so as to strictly observe the implementation of the minimum take home pay required under Section 48 of the General Provisions of FY 2018 GAA.

We recommended that Management secure from the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated and Liga ng mga Barangay sa Pilipinas the government official receipts for all payments made totaling P132,500 to establish proper financial accountability.
We recommended Management to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 on the prohibition against excessive and unnecessary expenditures.

We recommend the Management to strictly comply with the guidelines set forth in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 series of 2015 in the implementation of the Enhanced AICS program of DSWD. Also, we recommend that the MSWDO should ensure that authority to approve grants beyond P5,000.00 was actually delegated to them.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should observe the proper accounting entries of transferred funds, especially with respect to recognition of assets as provided under Paragraphs 76-79 of PPSAS 23 and the proper use of accounts provided in the revised chart of account for LGU particularly in use of the Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets account for infrastructure projects which are still in the process of construction. 

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate adjusting entries to recognize the CIP account. We have prepared a proforma accounting entries for inter-agency transferred funds for your reference and guide (see Annex B).


We recommend further that a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement be submitted to this Office for review.
